title: Democratic Leaders Want the Party to Moderate. Its Base Has Other Ideas.
summary: The primary victories of leftist Democratic congressional candidates in New York came as some leaders were urging the party to move to the center to broaden its appeal.

title: Why New York’s Democratic Establishment Fell to Team Mamdani
summary: Democratic leaders found that their traditional tools, from rallies to phone banks to big-name campaign events, were no match for the left’s ground game and messaging.

title: In New York Election Results, More Evidence of Eroding Support for Israel
summary: If the shift in public opinion continues, it could reshape one of the United States’ closest alliances.

title: Trump Cancels Plans to Sign a Bipartisan Housing Bill
summary: Also, Western Europe is sweltering. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: New Clues Point to Taylor Swift Wedding Event at M.S.G.
summary: The New York Times has confirmed that Taylor Swift has rented the sports arena for the July 4 weekend, suggesting plans for a days-long celebration.

title: Trump Stokes Chaos in Congress as He Huddles With the G.O.P.
summary: Hours before visiting the Capitol, the president scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill, condemning “bad Republicans” for resisting his demands to ram through new voting restrictions.

title: From ‘Terrible People’ to ‘Smart People’: The Trump-Led Right Rethinks Iran
summary: The president has sought to recast the Iranian government as he pursues a peace deal. But there are signs that a softening on Iran in the Republican Party goes well beyond him.

title: Trump Asks Congress for $88 Billion, Mostly for War With Iran
summary: The proposal was all but dead on arrival in the Senate, where it would need bipartisan support, and comes amid growing G.O.P. skepticism about the conflict.

title: Gay Marriage Is Dividing Republicans, Again
summary: A look at the backlash to same-sex marriage — its strength, seriousness and the reasons behind it.

title: Support Builds on the Right for Prosecuting Women Who Get Abortions
summary: Sentiment is shifting amid frustration in the antiabortion movement that more abortions are happening now than when Roe v. Wade fell.

title: As Blockbusters Loom, Monkey Business at the Supreme Court
summary: An unlikely trio of justices issued a slashing critique of plea bargains that included several references to orangutans.

title: NATO’s Leader Makes His Case to Trump for Preserving the Alliance
summary: Using charts, Secretary General Mark Rutte appeared to defuse the president’s anger by showing that European countries were “equalizing” defense spending with the U.S.

title: Visitors Look at the Reflecting Pool and Disagree on What They See
summary: People have long gathered in solidarity by the Reflecting Pool, but amid the turmoil of President Trump’s attempted repairs there is little unity to be found.

title: Federal Appeals Panel Rejects Trump’s Effort to Gather Voting Data From States
summary: The ruling of a three-judge appeals panel in Michigan was the most significant rebuke yet to the Department of Justice’s effort to find ineligible voters in state voter rolls.

title: Dianna Russini Was an N.F.L. ‘Insider.’ Was She Also Out of Bounds?
summary: Ms. Russini’s closeness to a coach, Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots, led to her breakup with The Athletic and its parent, The New York Times Company.

title: Los Angeles Names a New Schools Superintendent After Scandal
summary: Andres Chait is a district veteran who has helped keep peace with labor unions. He started in the classroom and now runs a district with hundreds of thousands of students.

title: President Narcissus and the Fetid Reflecting Pool
summary: Why the president’s latest renovation project is so irresistible and resonant.

title: Democrats Are Done With Caution
summary: Tuesday’s primary demonstrated the astonishing political power of Mamdani and of the Democratic Socialists of America.

title: California’s Plastic Law Goes to Court: Is It Too Strict or Too Lax?
summary: The sweeping legislation, fully enacted last month, has been challenged by Republican-led states. Environmental groups have their own complaints.
